title: `create-quiver` CLI
objective: Ship a one-command CLI that scaffolds Quiver into a target repo and replaces the current manual `docs-template/` folder copy.
description: The manual copy step is the clearest sign that Quiver is not yet plug-and-play. This slice implements the first official installer surface, so a user can bootstrap Quiver into either an existing repo or a new target directory without reverse-engineering the template layout.
must: Implement a `create-quiver` CLI entrypoint that can scaffold into a target directory or the current repo
Support passing the project name non-interactively and running the initialization flow after copying the packaged assets
Print clear next steps that point the user to the generated docs and the first slice workflow
not_included: Alternate installer channels such as Homebrew
acceptance: A user can run the documented one-command installer and receive a scaffolded Quiver project without manually copying folders
The CLI supports at least one non-interactive mode suitable for docs, demos, and CI
The resulting scaffold matches the same generated-project contract verified in the earlier specs
tests: CLI scaffold smoke test for new directory mode
CLI scaffold smoke test for existing repo mode
assumptions: The CLI can rely on packaged template assets rather than a live git checkout
The project name should be passable without prompts for automation scenarios
